summary: Every employee row can change manager membership, a direct-report count, or an average age, so exact output has a linear inspection lower bound; one indexed self-join and grouped scan matches it.

optimality.argument: An uninspected employee row may be the only direct report of a manager, may change an existing manager's count, or may change that manager's rounded average age. All E rows must therefore be considered. Looking up each non-null reports_to value through the unique employee_id index and updating one manager's count and age sum takes constant expected work per row, for O(E) total time.

replacement_checks.evidence: An adversary can alter the reports_to or age value of any uninspected employee and thereby change manager inclusion, reports_count, or average_age.
The self-join resolves each direct relationship once through the unique employee_id key, while grouped count and age-sum states are updated once per matching report.
Authored fixtures cover both public samples, one report, direct-versus-indirect hierarchy semantics, no managers, unordered input, upward-reporting managers, and averages that round up or down.
